title: Trump signs law yanking $9B from NPR, PBS, foreign aid
summary: National Public Radio headquarters on North Capitol Street in Washington, D.C., on Tuesday, July 15, 2025. (Photo by Jacob Fischler/States Newsroom) President Donald Trump signed into law Thursday the bill Congress passed earlier this month to revoke $9 billion in previously approved spending for pu...

title: Evacuation level downgraded to Level 1 “Ready” on the Middle and Lower Salcha River areas
summary: The Middle and Lower Salcha River Level 2 “Set” evacuation notices has been downgraded to a Level 1 “Ready” due to moderated fire activity and work completed by firefighters to protect the cabins and other sites from the Ninetyeight Fire (#218). All Salcha River area evacuation levels are now at Level 1 “Ready,” which means residents should be ready for...

title: Water Conservation Notice
summary: Water Conservation Notice The City of Cordova wants to inform residents that our water supply is currently low. While rain is expected soon, it will not be enough to restore the supply. Please continue to conserve water over the next several weeks. Your efforts make a difference. Water-saving tips:...

title: Reminder: Call for President’s Award Nominations
summary: Dear Delegates and Friends of AFN: Nominations for the President’s Awards are due by close of business on Friday, August 15, 2025 . title: Tlozhavun Fire declared out; crews continue fire and aviation operations on the Ptarmigan Complex
summary: Fire managers called the Tlozhavun Fire out yesterday after the fire was 100% contained and completely suppressed. A 38-person Type 4 management organization continues to strategically engage on the Ptarmigan Complex fires. Warmer and drier weather will move into the BLM Alaska Fire Service (BLM AFS) Upper Yukon Zone, which covers northeastern...

title: Special Session, Farmers Markets, and lots more!
summary: Dear friends and neighbors, The Farmers Markets are open and chock full of delicious, fresh produce! Markets provide access to healthy foods, produced right here in Alaska, by Alaskans. Produce freshly picked here is more nutritious than the already weeks-old food shipped in to our grocery stores. A...

title: Opinion: You can’t hold funding for Alaska public education hostage and expect it to improve
summary: The Anchorage Daily News published Senator Tobin’s commentary about education funding. title: Alaska appeals to US Education Department after failing funding test, with $80.8 million at stake
summary: Deena Bishop, commissioner of the Alaska Department of Education and Early Development, at a news conference with Gov. Mike Dunleavy on education funding on Friday, Jan. 31, 2025. (Photo by Corinne Smith/Alaska Beacon) The state of Alaska has filed an appeal with the U.S. Department of Education after federal officials said the state failed a test measuring the differences in funding between school districts, which put more than $80.8 million in federal aid at risk. Each year, the state receives federal funding called “impact aid” intended to compensate districts with federally owned lands, which reduce the tax base to support schools. The state can put that funding toward its own funding for schools — which the state has done in the...

title: Restraint, Diplomacy, and Restoring Arctic Exceptionalism
summary: Restoring Arctic exceptionalism could boost US-Russia ties, aid climate action, and harness Indigenous knowledge on ecosystem changes. The post Restraint, Diplomacy, and Restoring Arctic Exceptionalism appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .

title: AST DUI: Fairabnks [AK25072968]
summary: On 07/24/2025, the Alaska State Troopers conducted a traffic stop in Fairbanks. Troopers' investigation determined that Angel Marroquin committed the crime of DUI. Angel was arrested and transported to FCC.

title: Trump illegally withheld Head Start payments, government watchdog says
summary: Federal payments for Head Start this year were significantly behind schedule compared with 2024 and that violated the Impoundment Control Act, according to the nonpartisan Government Accountability Office. (Photo by SDI Productions via Getty Images) The Health and Human Services Department illegally withheld payments from Head Start for the first months of President Donald Trump’s term, a government watchdog reported Wednesday. HHS payments for Head Start this year were significantly behind schedule compared with 2024. That violated the Impoundment Control Act, a law governing the president’s duty to spend congressionally appropriated funds, according to a rep...

title: Wrangell lifts boil water advisory
summary: Wrangell’s new water treatment plant on Feb. 13, 2025. (Colette Czarnecki/KSTK) After four days of a boil water advisory in Wrangell, the borough lifted the notice and the need to conserve water on Wednesday afternoon. City employees flushed hundreds of thousands of gallons of clean water through th...

title: Alaska state senator, key vote on possible budget veto override, gets waiver from U.S. Army
summary: Sen. Forrest Dunbar, D-Anchorage, speaks in favor of Senate Bill 39, a bill to address payday loans, on Tuesday, April 15, 2025. (Photo by James Brooks/Alaska Beacon) Anchorage Democratic Sen. Forrest Dunbar will be able to attend the Aug. 2 special session of the Alaska Legislature, he said late Tuesday in a post on Facebook . Dunbar, a member of the National Guard, is deployed to Poland on active-duty service but received a federal waiver that will allow him to return to Alaska for legislative work. Dunbar’s attendance is critical for lawmakers who hope to override Gov. Mike Dunleavy’s decision to veto millions of dollars in public school fu...
